page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 360 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-standard-96 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng tests and the top 10% for Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Its single-core performance is also highly competitive, achieving top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. The instance excels in data processing tasks, with single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is consistently strong across both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations. For database workloads, it delivers strong performance in PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations.

The instance is highly capable for CPU-bound workloads, software compilation, and static web serving, where it achieves top-tier throughput. It also shows strong performance in large language model (LLM) inference for prompt processing and text generation using small to medium models, though performance drops to average levels when executing text generation on large 70B models. Due to the lack of local storage and GPUs, workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or hardware-accelerated graphics and deep learning training must rely on network-attached storage and external accelerators. This makes the c4-standard-96 best suited for high-density general-purpose applications, enterprise web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs and 360 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.3 GHz across 48 physical cores, it supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional data compression speeds. The instance excels in static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference for small to medium models. However, it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data-intensive operations. This configuration is highly efficient for enterprise web serving, database operations, and general-purpose compute workloads that demand high multi-threaded performance without the need for physical GPU acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 features 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 offers 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core compute performance.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance featuring advanced Intel Xeon processors, optimized for multi-threaded compute, web serving, and CPU-based inference.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 360 GB of RAM.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector math.
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and web serving.
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput data compression capabilities.
No local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached resources.
Optimized for enterprise applications, databases, and CPU-based LLM inference.
